description: ADSL performance comparison — Inc 10 initial vs Inc 10 optimized vs Inc 16 (per-tick observation cache with dirty invalidation)
measurement_notes: Seed 42, quiet_logs=true, Windows Python 3.14 reference host
Engine-only timings exclude export bundle and analytics generation
Full-run timings include ADR-009 bundle build with insights
Inc 16: SideObservationCache reused across agents until a state-mutating action applies

comparisons.interpretation: Workshop scenario faster; cache reuse helps when agents hold fire
Scale scenario ~2.3× faster engine-only vs Inc 10
Mega scenario ~2.8× faster engine-only; memory ~44 MB peak (engine-only)

profiling_summary.primary_bottleneck_inc10: Per-agent observation model_copy on every turn (~50% CPU on mega-scale)
profiling_summary.primary_bottleneck_inc16: Agent decision logic and audit trace construction (~35% CPU after cache)
inc16_optimizations: SideObservationCache with dirty invalidation on applied actions
Pre-built nodes_by_id / routes_by_id / endpoint indexes in observation context
NetworkIndex route endpoint indexes for engine mutations
